content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN. You want to find an integer that is not present in the array AAA.

Unfortunately, you only know addition of 222 numbers. So you will try to find an integer of the form X=Ai+AjX = A_i + A_jX=Ai​+Aj​ (1≤i,j≤N1 \le i, j \le N1≤i,j≤N), such that XXX does not appear in the array AAA.

If it is possible to find such an integer, print AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​. Here, AiA_iAi​ may be equal to AjA_jAj​, and even iii may be equal to jjj. If it is not possible, print −1-1−1.

If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​, the values chosen if it is possible, otherwise −1-1−1.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : In the first test case, you choose i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 to get X=−5+5=0X = -5 + 5 = 0X=−5+5=0 which is not present in the array.
Test Case 3 : You choose i=j=1i = j = 1i=j=1. X=10X = 10X=10 not present in the array.
